source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Stone Energy Corporation OCS-G 01665

actions_taken: Removed metal plate and replaced with manufactured sight glass
Operator grabbed fire extinguisher and extinguished flame
Operator used water hose to saturate insulation on exhaust
Construction crew installed flat bar around Compressor hatch
BSEE witnessed testing of LSL and PSH on vessel
Installed containment barrier around hatch cover
Lowered Level Safety Low approximately 4 inches
Ordered and installed sight glass next to LSL
event.summary: On 2-FEB-2015 at 0230 hours, a flash fire occurred on Stone Energy's OCS-G 01665 Main Pass 288-A Platform in the Gulf of Mexico. The Kimray Temperature Controller on the BAP-1500 Line Heater malfunctioned, causing excessive temperature rise above the normal 150 degrees setting. The Temperature Safety High (TSH) sensor failed to shut off fuel supply and inflow of combustible fluids. This caused Glycol and Antifreeze mixture to boil over and spill onto the lower deck, dripping onto the Compressor exhaust and igniting. The fire was extinguished by operators using a fire extinguisher and water hose. Contributing factors included a metal plate blocking the burner sight glass port, preventing visual inspection for abnormal conditions, and the Pressure Safety High (PSH) sensor also failing to function.

hazards.name: Flammable liquid and gas present on platform
High temperature equipment with combustible fluids

threats.name: Temperature controller malfunction causing excessive temperature
Safety instrumented system failure (TSH and PSH sensors)
Burner sight glass port blocked preventing visual inspection
Combustible fluid spillage onto ignition source

controls.name: Kimray Temperature Controller
controls.barrier_role: Primary control to maintain safe operating temperature
controls.lod_basis: First line of defense to prevent excessive temperature

supporting_text: The Operators later discovered that the 'Kimray Temperature Controller' was not working properly and caused the temperature to rise above the normal setting of 150 degrees.
Kimray Temperature Controller malfunctioned.

controls.name: Temperature Safety High (TSH) sensor
controls.barrier_role: Safety instrumented system to shut down vessel on high temperature
controls.lod_basis: Second line of defense to prevent excessive temperature by automatic shutdown

supporting_text: the (Primary Protection), Temperature Safety High (TSH) and Pressure Safety High (PSH) on the BAP-1500 Line Heater sensors on the fired components did not shut off the fuel supply and inflow of the combustible fluids.
Temperature Safety High (TSH) on BAP-1500 did not activate in order to shut down the vessel and all associated components.
TEMPURTURE SAFETY HIGH (TSH) DID NOT FUNTION AT THE TIME OF THE INCIDENT CAUSING HIGH TEMPURTURE IN THE VESSEL

controls.name: Pressure Safety High (PSH) sensor
controls.barrier_role: Safety instrumented system to shut down vessel on high pressure
controls.lod_basis: Second line of defense to prevent excessive pressure by automatic shutdown

supporting_text: Pressure Safety High (PSH) on the BAP-1500 Line Heater sensors on the fired components did not shut off the fuel supply
PRESSURE SAFETY HIGH (PSH) DID NOT FUNCTION AT THE TIME OF TH INCIDENT CAUSING HIGH PRESSURE IN THE VESSEL

controls.name: Burner sight glass port with visual inspection capability
controls.barrier_role: Enable visual inspection for abnormal conditions and maintenance
controls.lod_basis: First line of defense to detect abnormal conditions through visual inspection

supporting_text: the air intake of the natural draft burner port was blocked with the metal plate from when the vessel was shipped to the platform after previous repairs. With the metal plate covering the port, there was no way to view the burner for maintenance or to check for abnormal conditions such as 'hot spots' in the fire tube.
the sight glass was never installed posing a risk of rendering the Flame Arrestor (FA) on the air intake inoperable

controls.name: Flame Arrestor on air intake
controls.barrier_role: Prevent flame propagation through air intake
controls.lod_basis: First line of defense to prevent flame propagation

supporting_text: the sight glass was never installed posing a risk of rendering the Flame Arrestor (FA) on the air intake inoperable by allowing a path for air to enter the fire tube from a location other than the protected normal intake spot.

controls.name: Level Safety Low (LSL) sensor
controls.barrier_role: Monitor fluid level to prevent boil-over
controls.lod_basis: Second line of defense to prevent fluid overflow

supporting_text: The BSEE team asked the operators to test the Level Safety Low (LSL) and the Pressure Safety High (PSH) to ensure the components were in working condition; all components passed the test.
The Level Safety Low (LSL) was lowered approximately 4 inches to allow for level to come in service & Glycol to expand once heated.

controls.name: Containment barrier around hatch cover
controls.barrier_role: Prevent spillage of combustible fluids onto lower deck
controls.lod_basis: Recovery measure to contain spillage and prevent contact with ignition sources

supporting_text: Construction crew installed flat bar around the Compressor hatch to prevent any further leakage to the lower deck.
A containment barrier was installed around hatch cover to prevent any fluids from leaking onto the compressor exhaust.

controls.name: Manual fire suppression by operators
controls.barrier_role: Extinguish fire using handheld fire extinguisher and water hose
controls.lod_basis: Recovery measure to suppress fire after ignition

supporting_text: The Operator immediately shut the Compressor down, grabbed a fire extinguisher, and put the flame out.
used the water hose to saturate the insulation on the exhaust that was smoking.
TYPE OF FIREFIGHTING EQUIPMENT UTILIZED: X HANDHELD, X FIXED WATER

controls.name: Operator awareness and response procedures
controls.barrier_role: Detect abnormal conditions and initiate emergency response
controls.lod_basis: Recovery measure dependent on operator situational awareness and response

supporting_text: During the interviews with both Night Operators whom were involved in the flash fire incident, the Operators were explaining that they were setting up to test the Pressure Safety High Low (PSHL) pilots on the Compressor #1, as they were walking around the cooler they felt drops of what they thought were raindrops.
After they figured it was not raindrops, they ran upstairs and noticed the Line Heater was boiling over a Glycol and Antifreeze Mixture from the top hatch of the Line Heater.
The Operators shut the hatch of the Line Heater. Unsure of the cause at that time, the Operators shut the Line Heater down and secured the vessel.
By the time he was half way down the stairs, the Operator noticed that the insulation of the exhaust on the compressor was smoking. The Operator immediately shut the Compressor down

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
